trivia_bullets.text: Vile Aggregate's Ingest keyword — 'whenever this creature deals combat damage to a player, that player exiles the top card of their library' — is the drone-tier expression of the BFZ Eldrazi consumption engine. The mechanic represents the Eldrazi literally eating the substance of a plane: cards exiled by Ingest feed the Processors mechanic, which converts that exiled matter into usable energy, modeling an extractive pipeline at cosmological scale [WebSearch: BFZ Eldrazi Ingest-Processor dynamic; Scryfall: oracle_text].
sources: WebSearch: Battle for Zendikar Eldrazi devoid mechanic plane consumption lore 2015

trivia_bullets.text: Vile Aggregate's power is a star (*) equal to the number of colorless creatures controlled — making its strength scale with Eldrazi swarm density. This means the card mechanically rewards the same Eldrazi-flood strategy that the Ingest-Processor pipeline demands, creating tight internal cohesion within the BFZ Eldrazi theme. Artist: Chris Rallis [Scryfall]. EDHREC rank 19195 [Scryfall] — modestly more played than the Sentinel, likely because Trample + variable power creates a glass-cannon payoff in casual Eldrazi-tribal shells.

Vile Aggregate has no flavor text [Scryfall confirms flavor_text field is absent]. Its art depicts unnamed Eldrazi drones swarming a fallen prey figure — unnamed creatures consistent with the anti-confabulation rule: Vile Aggregate depicts generic Eldrazi drone swarm, not any named Eldrazi titan (Ulamog, Kozilek, or Emrakul), who appear on their own named cards.

Vile Aggregate is not Pauper-legal (uncommon), but is Pioneer- and Modern-legal [Scryfall: legalities]. Its Trample + Ingest combination was noted at the time of BFZ previews as a rare confluence: Ingest usually appears on smaller utility drones, while Trample is a finisher keyword. The combination was mechanically intentional — the aggregate-swarm creature that also devours the library mid-combat.
